patterns.pattern_name: Anxiety-like symptoms require differential separation from dysautonomia, respiratory, endocrine, metabolic, medication and arrhythmia mechanisms

mediator.name: mechanism_specific_physiological_signature
mediator.pathway: similar symptoms can arise from distinct physiological or psychiatric mechanisms
mediator.mechanism: panic respiratory instability; POTS orthostatic tachycardia; hyperthyroid adrenergic state; hypoglycemia; arrhythmia; stimulant/withdrawal effects

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Do not label tachycardia,dyspnea,tremor or dizziness as psychological anxiety by default; require mechanism-specific context and testing.

hypotheses.reason: HRV/orthostatic/capnography/endocrine/ECG markers can distinguish overlapping mechanisms when measured during symptoms.

evidence.summary: Anxiety-like symptoms overlap across panic disorder, POTS/OI, hyperventilation/hypocapnia, thyroid dysfunction, hypoglycemia, arrhythmias and medication effects; multimodal testing improves differential specificity.

key_sources.title: Differential Characterization of Anxiety-like Physiology: Panic, Dysautonomia, Endocrine, and Related Mechanisms
key_sources.relevant_finding: Reduced HRV is cross-diagnostic; orthostatic tachycardia/POTS can mimic/coexist with anxiety; capnography and endocrine panels help subtyping.
key_sources.limitations: No single marker perfectly distinguishes all mechanisms.

patterns.pattern_name: HRV is useful but non-specific across anxiety-like states

mediator.name: autonomic_imbalance
mediator.pathway: reduced vagal regulation correlates with anxiety-like symptom severity across diagnoses
mediator.mechanism: autonomic arousal, impaired vagal regulation, stress physiology

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Low HRV supports autonomic arousal/imbalance but cannot identify panic vs POTS vs endocrine/substance context alone.

hypotheses.reason: Reduced HRV is robust but cross-diagnostic.

evidence.summary: Reduced vagally mediated HRV is consistently replicated across anxiety states, but is not mechanism-specific.

key_sources.title: Differential Characterization of Anxiety-like Physiology: Panic, Dysautonomia, Endocrine, and Related Mechanisms
key_sources.relevant_finding: Reduced HRV is robust across anxiety states but requires context and additional measurements for differential inference.

limitations: HRV is sensitive to many confounders

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Anxiety-like symptoms are psychological by default.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: POTS, hyperventilation/hypocapnia, endocrine/metabolic states, arrhythmias and medications can produce similar symptoms.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Anxiety-like symptom cluster requiring differential mechanism testing before psychiatric attribution.
Non-specific autonomic arousal state often indexed by low HRV/high HR but requiring context.

research_gaps: Simultaneous HRV/capnography/endocrine monitoring during symptomatic episodes.
Medication/substance timing effects on anxiety-like measurements.

most_supported_patterns: Reduced HRV is robust but non-specific.
POTS/OI can mimic or coexist with anxiety.
Capnography adds value for hyperventilation/hypocapnia subtyping.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: Anxiety-like state uncertain until timing and mechanism markers are known.
Orthostatic, respiratory, endocrine, metabolic, arrhythmia and medication branches must be considered.
patterns_requiring_caution: Fatigue/anxiety post-COVID not always explained by autonomic dysfunction.
recommended_next_queries: Panic vs POTS real-time multimodal differentiation.
summary.overall_assessment: Anxiety-like physiology is a differential-inference phenotype, not a psychiatric conclusion; multimodal timing-aware measurement is required.

works_cited.title: Differential Characterization of Anxiety-like Physiology: Panic, Dysautonomia, Endocrine, and Related Mechanisms
